parameters.name: X-Gusto-API-Version
parameters.description: Determines the date-based API version associated with your API call. If none is provided, your application's [minimum API version](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/api-versioning#minimum-api-version) is used.

parameters.name: processing_statuses
parameters.description: Whether to include processed and/or unprocessed payrolls in the response, defaults to processed, for multiple attributes comma separate the values, i.e. `?processing_statuses=processed,unprocessed`

parameters.name: payroll_types
parameters.description: Whether to include regular and/or off_cycle payrolls in the response, defaults to regular, for multiple attributes comma separate the values, i.e. `?payroll_types=regular,off_cycle`

parameters.name: processed
parameters.description: Whether to return processed or unprocessed payrolls

parameters.name: include_off_cycle
parameters.description: Whether to include off cycle payrolls in the response

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Include the requested attribute in the response. The risk_blockers option will include submission_blockers and credit_blockers if applicable. The reversals option will include reversal payroll UUIDs if applicable. In v2023-04-01 totals are no longer included by default. For multiple attributes comma separate the values, i.e. `?include=totals,payroll_status_meta`.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Return payrolls whose pay period is after the start date

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Return payrolls whose pay period is before the end date. If left empty, defaults to today's date.

parameters.name: date_filter_by
parameters.description: Specifies which date field to use when filtering payrolls with start_date and end_date. This field applies only to regular processed payrolls and defaults to pay period if not provided.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page that is requested. When unspecified, will load all objects unless endpoint forces pagination.

parameters.name: per
parameters.description: Number of objects per page. For majority of endpoints will default to 25

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: A string indicating whether to sort resulting events in ascending (asc) or descending (desc) chronological order. Events are sorted by their `timestamp`. Defaults to asc if left empty.
